relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Jude writes to the called, beloved, and kept.
Mercy, peace, and love are multiplied.
The common salvation calls for guarding the entrusted trust.
Grace is not to be twisted into harm.
Memory warns through the wilderness story.
Leaving one's proper dwelling becomes warning.
Sodom and its neighbors are held as severe warning requiring careful audit.
Dreamlike arrogance defiles and rejects true lordship.
Even Michael answers accusation with restraint.
Ignorant slander corrupts itself.
Cain, Balaam, and Korah become warning-signs.
Self-shepherding emptiness hides inside love-feasts.
Unstable brilliance becomes wandering darkness.
Enoch's ancient witness is invoked.
Irreverent works and hard words are exposed.
Grumbling and flattery serve advantage.
Beloved ones remember the apostolic warnings.
Mockery follows irreverent desire.
Division without Spirit is named.
Build yourselves in holy trust and pray in holy Spirit.
Keep yourselves in Divine love while awaiting mercy.
Have mercy on those who doubt.
Rescue with mercy and caution.
The Divine can guard you unstumbling and present you in joy.
Glory, majesty, strength, and authority belong to the only Divine Savior through Jesus Messiah.

contemplative_note: Jude is fierce discernment held inside mercy: build in holy trust, pray in Spirit, keep in love, rescue carefully, and trust the One able to guard from stumbling.
